instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Benefits of Life Insurance
In India, many people are of the mindset that life insurance is not needed as long as they are fit and healthy. As per the Latest Economic Survey conducted by the Ministry of Finance, 3 out of 100 Indians are uninsured under the life insurance category1. We are the 10th largest life insurance market across the globe2. In order to increase life insurance penetration for the betterment of society, it is important to create awareness about life insurance by highlighting its benefits of life insurance.
Life insurance policies come in many types apart from a pure protection term life plan. Types of life insurance policies include term insurance, whole life insurance, endowment insurance, life insurance with retirement benefits, unit-linked investment plans, etc. Let us look at the advantages of life insurance to understand the importance of having a life insurance policy.
What are the Benefits of Life Insurance?
The following are the benefits of a life insurance policy:
Risk cover:The primary advantage of life insurance is it provides you with life cover. Life cover or risk cover is the sum assured that the life insurance policy pays to your loved ones in case of your untimely demise during the policy term. This acts like a financial cushion for your family to secure their future even when you are not around in this world. The death benefit is a lump sum of money that may be utilised for children’s education, paying off debts, or as an investment to meet day-to-day needs. It is important to avail an adequate amount of coverage, keeping in mind your family’s future financial needs.
Return on investment:Life insurance policies like endowment plans, ULIPs, and money-back plans provide returns on the money invested by you in the policy. Such plans give you life cover, as well as invest the premium, post deduction of applicable charges, as per the terms and conditions of the policy. Upon policy maturity, the amount that you invested is paid back along with returns in the form of maturity benefits.
Goal-based planning:A life insurance policy can be used as an ideal financial tool to meet your financial goals, such as children’s higher education, purchasing a new house, retirement planning, etc. There are various life insurance policies that you can choose based on your goal, risk appetite, and time horizon. You may opt for plans like ULIPs, endowment plans for saving for children’s higher education and pension plans for meeting your retirement needs. There are plans that come with assured income benefits too that can help you meet your regular expenses or compensate for the income loss due to accidents or disability.
Life insurance plans can serve your varied goals like:
Income replacement for your family
Purchasing a home, home renovation, etc.
Dream vacation
Children’s education
Retirement planning and pension benefits, etc.
Loan against the life insurance policy:There are some life insurance policies that provide you with the benefit of availing the loan against the policy value for meeting any urgent liquidity requirements. However, the amount of loan that you can avail of may vary depending on the sum assured or the cash value of the policy as well as the terms and conditions of the life insurance policy.
Tax benefits:The premiums of up to Rs. 1,50,000 a year that you pay for a life insurance policy qualify for tax deduction under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act, 1961, as per the provisions stated therein. The tax exemption on the lump sum benefits can be availed under Section 10(10D) of the Income Tax Act, 1961, as per the provisions stated therein.
Add-ons or riders:Life insurance policies come with many add-ons or riders that can be availed by paying an nominal additional premium. Some of the common riders that are offered in a life insurance policy are:
Accidental Death Benefit Rider:If the policyholder dies due to an accident, the beneficiary will get the additional sum assured for the accidental death.
Accidental Permanent Total and Partial Disability Benefit Rider:Due to an accident, if the policyholder suffers an injury leading to total/partial permanent disability or temporary disability, then the accidental disability rider pays out the benefit as an income replacement for the specified period in the life insurance policy. This is applicable for accidental disabilities during the policy term.
Critical Illness Benefit Rider:Critical illness benefit rider is an important life insurance benefit that offers coverage for fatal illnesses like paralysis, heart ailments, cancer, stroke, kidney ailments, etc. The number of critical illnesses covered under the rider may vary from one insurance company to another. The lumpsum benefit pre-defined in the rider is paid out on the diagnosis of any listed critical illness during the policy term.
Waiver of Premium Rider:Suppose the policyholder becomes unable to pay the future premiums of the life insurance policy due to an income loss caused by disability. In that case, the rider waives off the future premiums by keeping the policy benefits intact.
Family Income Benefit Rider:When the death benefit is paid to the beneficiary in the event of the insured’s death, this rider can work as a regular source of income for the loved ones for a specific period or years.
How do Life Insurance Plans Suit your Financial Needs?As we know, life insurance plans come with a variety of benefits, and address your diverse financial needs like :
Protection against the uncertainties
A life insurance plan with adequate coverage is an ideal financial backup for your loved ones when you are not around in this world to take care of their dreams. You can avail protection against unforeseen risks like death, disability, accident, and critical illness.
Management of risk
Having life insurance coverage may help you manage the risk of debt. It reduces the fear of inability to pay out the outstanding debt in case of your sudden demise. You may not pass on the burden of debt to your loved ones if you have adequate life insurance coverage.
Achieve long-term goals
Life insurance can serve as a long-term financial tool that helps you meet your long-term goals such as education of your child, retirement, saving for your spouse, home, children’s marriage, etc.
Tax saving
You can save tax by investing in life insurance products as you can claim deductions and exemptions under different of the Income Tax Act, 1961, as per the provisions stated therein.
Conclusion
In a nutshell, life insurance is an important part of financial planning that ensures the financial wellness of your loved ones even when you are not around. Secure your family’s future financial needs with adequate life insurance coverage.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. output.final_text:
Benefits of Life Insurance
In India, many people are of the mindset that life insurance is not needed as long as they are fit and healthy. As per the Economic Survey 2022, conducted by the Ministry of Finance, only 3 out of 100 Indians are insured under the life insurance category1. We are the 10th largest life insurance market across the globe2. In order to increase life insurance penetration, it is important to create awareness about life insurance by highlighting its benefits.
Life insurance policies come in many types. Types of life insurance policies include term insurance, whole life insurance, endowment insurance, life insurance with retirement benefits, Unit Linked Insurance Plans (ULIPs), etc. Let us look at the benefits of life insurance to understand the importance of having a life insurance policy.
What are the Benefits of Life Insurance?
The following are some of the benefits of a life insurance policy:
1. Risk cover:
The primary advantage of life insurance is it provides you with life cover. Life cover or risk cover is the sum assured that the life insurance policy pays to your nominees in case of your untimely demise during the policy term. This acts like a financial cushion for your family to secure their financial future even when you are not around in this world. The death benefit is a lump sum of money that may be utilised for children’s education, paying off debts, or as an investment to meet day-to-day needs. It is important to avail an adequate amount of coverage, keeping in mind your family’s future financial needs.
2. Return on investment:
Life insurance policies like endowment plans, and ULIPs, provide returns on the money invested by you in the policy. ULIPs give you life cover, as well as invest the premium in different market-linked funds, post deduction of applicable charges, as per the terms and conditions of the policy for earning market linked returns. In endowment policies, upon policy maturity, the amount that you invested is paid back along with returns in the form of maturity benefits.
3. Goal-based planning:
A life insurance policy can be used as a financial tool to meet your financial goals, such as children’s higher education, purchasing a new house, retirement planning, etc. There are various life insurance policies that you can choose based on your goal, risk appetite, and time horizon. You may opt for plans like ULIPs, endowment plans for saving for children’s higher education, your dream home, etc. and pension plans for meeting your retirement needs. There are plans that come with assured* income benefits that can help you meet your regular expenses. Life insurance plans can help you in serving your varied goals, among others, like:
○ Income replacement for your family ○ Purchasing a home, home renovation, etc. ○ Dream vacation ○ Children’s education ○ Retirement planning and pension benefits, etc.
4. Loan against the life insurance policy:
There are some life insurance policies that provide you with the benefit of availing the loan against the policy value for meeting any urgent liquidity requirements. However, the amount of loan that you can avail of may vary depending on the sum assured or the cash value of the policy and the terms and conditions of the policy.
5. Tax benefits:
The premiums of up to Rs. 1,50,000 a year that you pay for a life insurance policy qualify for deduction under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act, 1961, as per the provisions stated therein under the old tax regime. Tax exemption on the death benefits and maturity benefits can also be availed under Section 10(10D) of the Income Tax Act, 1961, subject to the satisfaction of provisions stated therein. However, from January 20213, it was announced that ULIPs bought thereafter would not be exempted from income tax under section 10(10D) if the annual premium exceeded Rs. 2.5 lakhs per annum.
Also, life insurance policies (other than ULIP) with an annual premium of more than Rs. 5 lakhs4 per annum, issued from 1st April 2023 as per Union Budget 2023$, would not be exempted from income tax under section 10(10D) of Income Tax Act, 1961.
6. Add-ons or riders:
Life insurance policies come with add-on riders that can be availed by paying a nominal additional premium. Some of the common riders that are offered in a life insurance policy are:
○ Accidental Death Benefit Rider: If the policyholder dies due to an accident, the beneficiary will get the additional sum assured over and above the sum assured under the base plan, as per the rider terms and conditions.
○ Accidental Permanent Total and Partial Disability Benefit Rider: Due to an accident, if the policyholder suffers an injury leading to total/partial permanent disability , then the accidental disability rider offers financial assistance in such contingencies, which the policyholder can use to manage treatment or other expenses. This is applicable for accidental disabilities occurring during the policy term and is subject to rider terms and conditions.
○ Critical Illness Benefit Rider: Critical illness benefit rider is an important life insurance benefit that offers coverage for fatal illnesses like heart ailments, cancer, renal failure, etc. The number of critical illnesses covered under the rider may vary from one insurance company to another. The rider pays out a lump sum benefit on the diagnosis of any of the critical illnesses listed under the said rider during the policy term.
○ Waiver of Premium Rider: Suppose the policyholder is unable to pay the future premiums of the life insurance policy due to an income loss caused by disability or critical illness. In that case, the rider waives off the future premiums by keeping the policy benefits intact, subject to the terms & conditions of the rider.
How do Life Insurance Plans Suit your Financial Needs? As we know, life insurance plans come with a variety of benefits, and help in addressing your diverse financial needs like :
1. Financial Protection against the uncertainties
A life insurance plan with adequate coverage is a suitable l financial backup for your loved ones when you are not around in this world to take care of their dreams. You can avail protection against unforeseen risks like death, disability, accident, and critical illness. 2. Achieve long-term goals
Life insurance can serve as a long-term financial tool that helps you meet your long-term goals such as education of your child, retirement, saving for your spouse, home, children’s marriage, etc. 3. Tax saving
You can save tax by investing in life insurance products as you can claim deductions and exemptions under different sections of the Income Tax Act, 1961, as per the provisions stated therein. Conclusion
In a nutshell, life insurance is an important part of financial planning that helps in ensuring the financial wellness of your loved ones even when you are not around. Secure your family’s future financial needs with adequate life insurance coverage.
Disclaimer:
IN THIS POLICY, THE INVESTMENT RISK IN INVESTMENT PORTFOLIO IS BORNE BY THE POLICYHOLDER. Investment in ULIPs is subject to risks associated with the capital markets.
